mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved earnings expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2005 retail sales at 344895M suggests sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% and continues rising, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This signals future earnings headwinds through higher borrowing costs and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.79% as of April 2005 indicates tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates tight labor markets and wage pressure. This interaction signals inflation risk and Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% and PAYEMS at 133293K suggest tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint signal of low UNRATE and strong PAYEMS growth fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 as of March 2005 reflects manufacturing strength
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) interact with real GDP growth (GDPC1) to signal construction-led demand cycles. When both are rising, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on employment and consumer wealth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1800K units annualized and GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1837K and GDPC1 at 11078.2B indicate housing-led expansion
failure_condition: Joint signal of strong HOUST and GDPC1 fails to predict positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy constraints. When combined with FEDFUNDS already above 2.5%, the Fed faces stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates compress equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 193.2 and FEDFUNDS at 2.79% indicate inflation-constrained policy
failure_condition: Joint signal of high inflation and high rates fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% signals economic weakness and reduced corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk and equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% as of April 2005 provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: Rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals broad-based economic expansion. When both accelerate simultaneously, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 and PAYEMS at 133293K indicate synchronized expansion
failure_condition: Joint signal of strong INDPRO and PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
